summary: Fei-Fei Li discusses her journey from creating ImageNet to founding World Labs, emphasizing that spatial intelligence—understanding and generating the 3D world—is the next critical frontier for AI. She draws inspiration from evolutionary biology, advocates for intellectual fearlessness in researchers and founders, and advises academia to focus on fundamental problems rather than competing with industry's compute power.

principles.statement: AGI will not be complete without spatial intelligence.
principles.rationale: Vision and spatial understanding triggered the evolutionary arms race of animal intelligence. Understanding, navigating, and interacting with the 3D world is a fundamental pillar of intelligence that goes beyond the 1D nature of language.
principles.source_quote: To me, AGI will not be complete without spatial intelligence. And I want to solve that problem.

principles.statement: Academia should pursue 'north star' problems that industry cannot simply brute-force.
principles.rationale: Industry has vastly more compute and data resources. Academia should focus on interdisciplinary AI, theory, explainability, and small data rather than competing on scale.
principles.source_quote: I would recommend you to look for those north stars that are not on a collision course of problems that industry can solve better using better compute, better data, and team science.

principles.rationale: As technology advances and integrates into society, it becomes a human problem, not just an industrial one. We must ensure it helps humanity.
principles.source_quote: Humanity will always advance our technology, but we cannot lose our humanity.

frameworks.name: Finding North Star AI Problems
frameworks.when_to_apply: When looking for the next fundamental research direction or paradigm shift in AI.
steps: Look to evolution and brain science for inspiration.
Identify capabilities that took evolution the longest to develop or triggered massive leaps in intelligence (e.g., vision taking 540M years).
Target problems that are 'bordering delusional' and haven't been solved because they are fundamentally hard.
frameworks.source_quote: A lot of my inspiration actually come from evolution as well as brain science. I find myself in many moments of my career where I'm looking for the next north star problem to solve. I ask myself, what is what evolution has done or what brain development has done.

mental_models.name: The Evolutionary Timeline of Intelligence
mental_models.description: Using the biological timeline of evolution to gauge the complexity and importance of AI capabilities. For example, language took less than a million years to evolve, while vision took 540 million years and sparked the Cambrian explosion of intelligence. This implies spatial intelligence is vastly more complex and foundational than language.
mental_models.example: Justifying the difficulty and importance of building 3D world models (spatial intelligence) over 1D Large Language Models by pointing out that vision set off the evolutionary arms race half a billion years ago.

mental_models.name: The Continuum of World Modeling
mental_models.description: Viewing spatial intelligence applications on a fluid spectrum between pure generation and real-world reconstruction.
mental_models.example: If you dial all the way to generation, you get gaming and the metaverse. If you dial all the way to the real world, you get robotics.

heuristics: Hire for intellectual fearlessness—the courage to embrace something hard and go all in.
If it's easy, somebody else has solved it.
Forget about what others think of you. Just hunker down and build.
Don't overindex on being a minority; focus on learning, doing, and creating.

signature_quotes.text: To me, AGI will not be complete without spatial intelligence. And I want to solve that problem.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing her motivation for founding World Labs and moving beyond LLMs.

signature_quotes.text: My entire career is going after problems that are just so hard, bordering delusional.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why she chose to tackle spatial intelligence despite the lack of readily available 3D data.

signature_quotes.text: Forget about what you have done in the past. Forget about what others think of you. Just hunker down and build. That is my comfort zone.
signature_quotes.context: Describing her love for being an entrepreneur and starting from ground zero.

signature_quotes.text: Humanity will always advance our technology, but we cannot lose our humanity.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why she founded the Human-Centered AI Institute at Stanford.
Describing the unifying trait among her most legendary students and her hiring criteria.

signature_quotes.text: If it's easy, somebody else has solved it.
signature_quotes.context: Embracing the extreme difficulty of building 3D world models.

anti_patterns.description: Academia trying to compete with industry on scale-driven AI problems.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Industry has vastly superior compute, data, and team science resources. Academia will lose if it tries to brute-force problems that industry is already scaling.

anti_patterns.description: Starting a startup purely out of curiosity.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Unlike grad school, which is designed for pure curiosity, a startup requires a focused commercial goal; otherwise, investors will be unhappy.

anti_patterns.description: Over-indexing on being a minority or the only person of your background in the room.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distracts from the core mission of learning, doing, and creating.
